key_insight: Foundation ML interatomic potentials can capture simple geometric order in correlated perovskites, but their performance drops sharply as the order becomes more symmetry-sensitive or purely electronic.
engineering_relevance: This work shows where off-the-shelf ML potentials are reliable for accelerated atomistic simulation of correlated oxides and where DFT or specialized training is still needed.
method: Ran 1 ns molecular dynamics on 80- and 160-atom supercells
Compared three foundation potentials against three low-temperature order types in LaMnO3, LaCoO3, and NdNiO3
evidence_signal: The authors evaluated three foundation potentials on three correlated ABO3 oxides and found they succeeded for NdNiO3’s scalar order, partially captured LaMnO3’s vector bond order, and failed on LaCoO3’s spin-state crossover.
watch_item: Off-the-shelf foundation MLIPs may miss local multiplet/spin-state physics even when they reproduce structural magnitudes, so validation is essential before using them for correlated oxides.
paper_title: Bond, orbital and spin order in d4/d6/d7 perovskite oxides: successes and limitations of foundation interatomic potentials
